title: CLAUDE.md
text: authoritative full-suite gate project : Expected: FAIL 0 / ERROR 0 / SKIP 0 / PASS N (N = 1583 2026-05-17 vignette-review; count grows — matters 0/0/0). devtools::test() equivalent (sets NOT_CRAN=true ). ⚠ Proxy trap — fooled (corrected 2026-05-17, empirically verified): - raw Rscript -e 'testthat::test_dir()/test_file()' NOT_CRAN → skip_on_cran() fires → every shinytest2 browser test silently SKIPs. “green, SKIP n” gate. - devtools::check(... ---cran) make skip_on_cran() fire (devtools runs check subprocess NOT_CRAN=true). browser fixtures instead cleanly SKIP via boot_vignette_app()’s source-root guard (DESCRIPTION .Rcheck sandbox). check run shows e2e file ERRORing (R CMD check found ERRORs, pkgload “DESCRIPTION found”), guard missing/broken — harness defect, product failure, never gate. - NOT_CRAN=true / devtools::test() runs browser tests (source tree present → 0 SKIP). alone gate. Never claim suite green, merge branch, mark step done, clear context skipping/erroring harness. gates: devtools::check(document = FALSE, manual = FALSE, args = c("---cran","---manual")) → 0 errors / 0 warnings (-design NOTEs expected: CRAN incoming “new submission”, .git, top-level CONTEXT.md); e2e browser file cleanly SKIPs via source-root guard — non-zero-error check means guard regressed, product failure (see proxy-trap block ). Docs: source("dev/build-pkgdown.R"); build_pkgdown_clean() → clean. Browser e2e needs shinytest2 + chromote + Chrome; absent → clean -skip acceptable must reported, hidden. Every plan project takes Definition Done block. Audit/enforce plan /implementable <plan-path>.

title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: L1 — --one: standalone entry points build run whole app: ptr_app() (single formula), ptr_app_grid() (multiple formulas). user writes formula, gets running Shiny app. Avoid: “simple API”, “quick mode”. L2 — Embed default layout: “Plug ggpaintr Shiny app, accept default layout (fluidPage → sidebar controls + main plot/code/error), optionally restyle via CSS.” L2 entry points self-contained pair ptr_ui() / ptr_server() (formerly ptr_module_ui / ptr_module_server — renamed ADR 0006; ptr_server() single public server entry, used L2 L3) shared pair ptr_shared_ui() / ptr_shared_server() (multi-plot cross-module sharing). non-ptr_ui/ptr_server split (ptr_controls_ui/ptr_outputs_ui) removed; shared pair orthogonal stays L2 public API. Avoid: “split”, “embedding API” (ambiguous L2 L3); “module pair” / “ptr_module_*” (renamed — say ptr_ui/ptr_server); reading trimmed split excluding shared pair. L3 — layout (UI-side split) — LOCKED 2026-05-16, amended 2026-05-18 (ADR 0006): L3 UI-side split : anything beyond default layout — break ptr_ui’s layout, place built-panes individually, replace pane widget, render path (plotly::renderPlotly() + plotlyOutput() instead ptr_ui_plot()). Composed bare pieces; L3 user supplies Shiny (optionally ptr_ui_page). L3 server-side concept. server always single public ptr_server(formula, id = NULL, …) — identical L2 L3; everything registered server-side; piece corresponding UI -op; built-plot/code/error read state$runtime(). Custom render = extract components state returned ptr_server() (ADR 0006; supersedes vignette’s “needs nothing beyond L2”, project-headless-removed memory’s “demoted L2 subsection” clause, ADR 0005’s “custom render owning render path” framing — -headless / -testServer--feature prohibition still stands). still L3 (place output widget) separate level server pattern: one server entry; read state$runtime()$plot return value. Canonical custom-render pattern: state <- ptr_server(formula, id) (inside host server, user’s widget shiny::NS(id)("my_plot") UI), output$my_plot <- plotly::renderPlotly(state$runtime()$plot). user-authored moduleServer wrapping ggpaintr engine public bare engine — namespacing handled ptr_server() . state server-side — never reachable UI code. state$server_ns_fn/ui_ns_fn internal plumbing, public — never documented user escape hatch. (Reason ADR 0006: single server entry custom render extracts returned state; old ptr_server-inside---moduleServer justification withdrawn internal-conclusion stands.) ptr_ui rebuilt L3 API: internal composition bare pieces + self-contained shell + combinators + (single-formula) auto inline shared section. ptr_server_internal (former exported ptr_server 4-arg engine) unexported plumbing — L1/L2/L3 surface, never recommended users. Avoid: “headless” (non-Shiny path — see CLAUDE.md / project memory), “custom rendering level” / “L3 server pattern” (custom render UI-side L3 fed one server entry, level server pattern), “render path” server concept, calling ptr_server “module” server implying sibling non-module server, telling users wire custom outputs state$server_ns_fn wrap bare engine moduleServer.

what: Shared placeholders — two distinct UI surfaces
title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: Shared placeholder: placeholder annotated … (shared = "<key>"). Every occurrence key — across aes, pipeline stages, (multi-plot) across formulas — driven one widget lockstep. Valid single-formula case (one key used multiple spots one plot) multi-formula case (one key across plots). Avoid: “linked input”, “global parameter”. shared section vs shared panel choice decided per key many formulas reference (partition rule — target design, yet implemented): shared key referenced exactly one formula → formula’s shared section. shared key referenced ≥2 formulas → one shared panel. Example: f1 = sharedA + sharedA + sharedB, f2 = sharedC + sharedC + sharedB ⟹ f1’s section holds sharedA, f2’s section holds sharedC, standalone panel holds sharedB. Shared section (inline, formula-local): block shared-key widgets rendered inside one formula’s control panel, keys occur formula. Namespaced via controls’ ns (<id>-shared_<key>); driven formula’s ptr_server() (single public server entry) — top-level ptr_shared_server(). Automatic (formula formula-local shared keys gets one; user-triggered). Today governed internal render_shared_section flag (whole-section /, per-key partition). Avoid: calling “panel”; assuming knows formulas. Shared panel (standalone, cross-formula): single page-level shiny::wellPanel() built ptr_shared_ui(formulas, …), holding keys referenced ≥2 formulas. Uses global, un-namespaced ids (shared_<key>, ptr_shared_draw_all, ptr_shared_errors); exactly one per page; server counterpart ptr_shared_server() must run top level, never inside moduleServer(id). Avoid: calling “section”; assuming can namespaced duplicated per module; assuming currently filters cross-formula keys (today takes keys).

title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: Shared coordinator (obj) — LOCKED 2026-05-16: single pure, non-reactive object built full formula set, consolidating shared config: ptr_shared(formulas, shared_ui = list(), ui_text = NULL, expr_check = TRUE, draw_all_label = "Draw ") → obj. (expr_check needed translates formulas find shared keys.) computes partition (per key: formula-local → formula’s section; ≥2 formulas → panel) single source truth UI server can never disagree. Consumed exactly three functions, take obj (duplicated config): - ptr_shared_panel(obj, css = NULL) — L2 self-contained panel (css = L2 restyle hook). - ptr_ui_shared_panel(obj) — L3 bare panel (css; L3 shell owns assets). - ptr_shared_server(obj) — reactive ptr_shared_state bundle, unchanged downstream ptr_server(shared_state=). Avoid: deriving partition independently anywhere else; treating obj reactive; putting shared_ui/ui_text config panel functions. Shared usage instance count — LOCKED 2026-05-16: - One ggpaintr instance ⟹ shared=obj; shared widgets auto-render instance’s inline shared section. coordinator, panel — ever. (Single-instance “promote standalone panel prominence” considered rejected.) - Multiple instances ⟹ user must build obj <- ptr_shared(formulas=list(…)); cross-formula keys (≥2) go one ptr_shared_panel(obj); formula-local keys still render instance’s inline section. - Therefore ptr_shared / ptr_shared_panel / ptr_shared_server strictly multi-instance API; single-instance embedders never see . old ptr_ui(shared_panel=) boolean removed — routing governed solely presence shared=obj + count-based partition. Shared coordinator consumers — exactly two roles, never : - ptr_ui_controls(id, formula, shared = obj) — renders layer controls plus formula’s formula-local shared section, excluding obj’s panel-owned keys. (Decision 2026-05-16: section folded controls piece, separate placeable piece.) - shared-panel piece — renders exactly obj’s ≥2-formula keys. - pieces (ptr_ui_plot/ptr_ui_error/ptr_ui_code/header) shared-agnostic — never take obj. - Single-formula L3, panel → omit obj; controls piece renders formula’s shared keys (every key formula-local definition).

title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: ptr_<x> = L2 self-contained (owns .ptr-app + asset bundle; drop host app; shell remember). ptr_ui_<x> = L3 bare (emits widgets; L3 user supplies Shiny / shell). rule governs entire api-audit rename. earlier ptr_embed_* idea dead (superseded). L2 self-contained pair ptr_ui/ptr_server (renamed ptr_module_ui/ptr_module_server ADR 0006, voids ADR 0004/0005’s sealed-bundle name exception). now regular ptr_<x> self-contained names; ptr_server single public server entry (sibling “module” server exists, “module” qualifier dropped misleading). L2 public surface (self-contained, ptr_ui_ prefix): ptr_ui / ptr_server (L2 entry; ptr_server also single public server entry L3), ptr_shared_panel (cross-formula panel UI), ptr_shared (coordinator) / ptr_shared_server. non-ptr_ui/ptr_server split ptr_controls_ui/ptr_outputs_ui removed. L3 public surface (bare): ptr_ui_controls, ptr_ui_plot, ptr_ui_error, ptr_ui_code, ptr_ui_header, ptr_ui_shared_panel, plus combinators (). Pieces truly orthogonal — ptr_ui_plot(id) error=/code_toggle= flags; behavior added combinators . ptr_ui_page(…, page, css) optional, L3-shell convenience — never L2 path; L3 users may instead write bare Shiny. owns one .ptr-app scope + (deduped htmlDependency) asset bundle via internal ptr_assets(). Combinator — LOCKED 2026-05-16: ptr_ui_* function takes already-built bare pieces returns wired behavior. Pure DOM-structure helpers; server coupling (server registers ptr_plot/ptr_error/ptr_code regardless). bundled JS (inst/www/ggpaintr-ui.js) drives toggle purely DOM-locally .ptr-output / .ptr-code-toggle / .ptr-code-window classes — combinators just emit markup. - ptr_ui_inline_error(plot, error) — wraps plot piece + error piece .ptr-output card error renders inline. - ptr_ui_toggle_code(plotish, code) — wraps plot-ish tag + code piece code hides behind </> toggle. - Nestable: ptr_ui_toggle_code(ptr_ui_inline_error(ptr_ui_plot(id), ptr_ui_error(id)), ptr_ui_code(id)). - Removed/superseded model: composites ptr_ui_code_toggle, ptr_ui_plot_code, ptr_ui_outputs; error=/code_toggle= flags ptr_ui_plot; ADR 0004’s locked ptr_ui_outputs ≡ ptr_ui_plot_code(error=TRUE) contract; earlier--session include_error_panel rename + default-flip. ADR 0005 records supersession. default L2 layout (plot + inline error + toggle code) lives inside ptr_ui, composed combinators. Asset escape hatch: Exported ptr_ui_assets(css) — manual asset injector, --ptr_ui_page-contract roots (navbarPage, bslib/BS5). part normal L2/L3 use.

title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: L1 entry point internally composes pieces L3 user composes hand; L2 default-layout middle. shared placeholder surfaces shared section (single-formula, inline controls) shared panel (multi-formula, standalone, global ids) — never key page. shared panel pairs 1:1 top-level ptr_shared_server(); bare pieces / ptr_ui bundle pair single ptr_server() matching id. partition rule decides ownership, just placement: one widget key — including var (shared = …) consumer’s column picker — rendered server-bound whoever owns surface. formula-local key owned one instance’s server; panel key (≥2 formulas) owned standalone panel + top-level ptr_shared_server(). host server owns panel keys; never reaches formula-local key. Single-instance panel, instance owns every shared key. page shell contains bare pieces; embed composite (conceptually) bare piece already wrapped shell.

title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: “shared panel” used inline single-formula section standalone multi-formula wellPanel. Resolved: distinct — shared section (inline, render_shared_section) vs shared panel (standalone, global ids, top-level server). design doc must say . “L2 split” vs “L3 pieces”: ptr_controls_ui/ptr_outputs_ui (self-wrapping composites) vs ptr_ui_* (bare). Earlier audit notes mislabeled cross-layer renames mechanical. redesign may delete composite layer L2 entirely. “custom render path” — Resolved (ADR 0006, 2026-05-18): custom render L3 (UI-side), done extracting components state returned single public ptr_server(). server-side concept, separate level, user-wrapped engine. “empty shared panel” — multi-instance app every shared key formula-local (≥2-formula key) leaves panel nothing show. Resolved (grill 2026-05-17): valid configuration, error — panel must render nothing (empty “Shared controls” box, orphan Draw-); instance still renders inline section, top-level ptr_shared_server() stays safe -op. “binds formula-local var (shared=)” — standalone-panel server found also binding formula-local consumer pickers (global ids), contradicting “owning instance binds ”. Resolved (grill 2026-05-17): host server binds panel keys; owning instance binds every formula-local key (consumer value). One partition, one owner, double-bind.

title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: Embedder: “’ll drop ptr_shared_ui() module’s sidebar every plot gets shared control.” Maintainer: “— shared panel uses global ids one top-level ptr_shared_server(); N copies collide. One standalone panel per page. one formula, don’t want panel — want inline shared section (render_shared_section = TRUE).”

title: Customization
text: Every label, placeholder, help string generated UI comes deep-merged copy-rules tree. ggpaintr ships set defaults; override subset passing ui_text = entry point. keys spell change — everything else stays built-value.

title: Customization
text: first three sections flat: one fixed lookup path per UI slot. last three nested keyword — , layers, layer name × keyword × parameter — letting scope override single placeholder, single parameter, single layer.

title: Customization
text: runtime needs copy control (widget rendered one placeholder occurrence one layer), deep-merges three rules order: defaults[[keyword]] — general. params[[param_key]][[keyword]] — keyed surrounding parameter name. layers[[layer_name]][[keyword]][[param_key]] — keyed layer placeholder lives . later rule wins fields specifies; fields omits fall back earlier rule. UI slots (chrome, upload widget, layer checkbox) resolution path fixed — override either replaces leaf field .

title: Customization
text: ptr_ui_text() validates partial override returns fully-merged ptr_ui_text object. Passing bare list (without wrapping ptr_ui_text()) works — entry points call internally — using explicitly catches typos section keys registration time rather render time. ptr_resolve_ui_text(component, keyword, param, layer_name, ui_text) lower-level lookup runtime uses fetch resolved copy single UI slot. Embedders writing custom UIs mix ggpaintr-driven labels copy can call directly; users need .
code: custom_text <- ptr_ui_text(list(   shell = list(     title       = list(label = "Iris explorer"),     draw_button = list(label = "Render")   ),   params = list(     x     = list(var  = list(label = "X variable")),     title = list(text = list(label = "Plot heading"))   ) ))  ptr_app(   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = var, color = var)) +      geom_point() +      labs(title = text)",   ui_text = custom_text )

title: Customization
text: ggpaintr ships bundled stylesheet (ggpaintr.css) stable, BEM-style class vocabulary ptr-* prefix. Three customization surfaces, order invasive :

what: Surface 1 — class hooks ggpaintr already emits
title: Customization
text: Every region default shell carries stable class can target CSS. vocabulary BEM (block, block__element, block--modifier) committed surface; future releases silently rename .

what: Surface 2 — injecting your own stylesheet with css =
title: Customization
text: Every self-contained entry point (ptr_app(), ptr_app_grid(), ptr_ui(), ptr_shared_panel()) accepts css = argument — optional L3 page shell ptr_ui_page(), ptr_ui_assets(css =) escape hatch non-ptr_ui_page() roots: character vector paths .css files. file registered static resource linked bundled stylesheet, rules win equal specificity. Relative url(...) references resolve file’s directory, can ship images next stylesheet. minimal -theme.css recolours title bar tightens card padding: css = argument validates path: missing files non-.css extensions abort construction time clear message — typos surface first browser load.
code: ptr_app(   "ggplot(mtcars, aes(var, var)) + geom_point()",   css = "my-theme.css" ) .ptr-app__header { background: #1f2937; color: #f9fafb; } .ptr-app__title  { font-family: "Inter", sans-serif; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ptr-card__body  { padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; }

title: Customization
text: .ptr-* table committed contract. Outside : Internal markup details — element order, nesting depth, descendant tag names — may change releases even class names stay. Style class, structural selectors like .ptr-card > div:nth-child(2). Classes emitted upstream Shiny, shinyWidgets, bslib (.shiny-input-container, .selectize-control, .form-control, .btn-primary, …) belong packages, ggpaintr — stability governed . Anything matching ^ptr_ id (Shiny input/output id), class; styling Shiny ids allowed brittle, since ids namespaced host modules.

title: Customization
text: CSS alone enough — want different page shell, theme system like bslib, layout map onto ptr_app()’s defaults — write thin wrapper around public ggpaintr primitives. non-module split (ptr_controls_ui() / ptr_outputs_ui()) removed L2/L3 redesign (ADR 0005); single-formula wrapper now composes bare L3 pieces plus scaffolding need: ptr_ui_controls(formula, id = NULL, ...) — layer picker, per-layer parameter panels, “Update plot” button. bare (emits widgets, wrapper, assets), wrapper supplies themed scope — shiny::tags$div(class = "ptr-app", ...) — bundled stylesheet via ptr_ui_assets() (self-deduping page-wide htmlDependency). output stack — ptr_ui_toggle_code(ptr_ui_inline_error(ptr_ui_plot(id), ptr_ui_error(id)), ptr_ui_code(id)): plot card, inline error, slide-generated-code window, composed bare panes two DOM-combinators. nested call byte--byte ptr_app() / ptr_ui() render internally. ptr_server(formula, id = NULL, ...) — server side. Forwards ui_text / checkbox_defaults / expr_check / safe_to_remove / envir [ptr_init_state()] wires reactivity, including shared widgets declared var(shared = "...") inside formula. package ships one worked example exported function, ptr_app_bslib(). recommended primary entry point — ptr_app() ptr_app_grid() . purpose demonstrate wrapper pattern. entire source short enough read one screen: Everything ggpaintr-specific source public API, save one internal ptr_resolve_ui_text() title lookup — wrapper can equally hard-code title string. non-ggpaintr pieces bslib::page_sidebar() shell theme = passthrough bslib::bs_theme(); belong bslib, ggpaintr. wrapper free expose downstream-library args alongside ggpaintr ones forwards.
code: ptr_app_bslib <- function(formula,                           envir = parent.frame(),                           ui_text = NULL,                           checkbox_defaults = NULL,                           expr_check = TRUE,                           safe_to_remove = character(),                           theme = NULL) {   if (!requireNamespace("bslib", quietly = TRUE)) {     rlang::abort(       "Package 'bslib' is required for ptr_app_bslib(). Install it with install.packages(\"bslib\")."     )   }   if (is.null(theme)) {     theme <- bslib::bs_theme(version = 5, bootswatch = "flatly")   }   id <- "ptr"   title <- ptr_resolve_ui_text("title", ui_text = ui_text)$label %||%     "ggpaintr"      ui <- bslib::page_sidebar(     title = title,     theme = theme,     sidebar = bslib::sidebar(       title = "Controls",       # ptr_controls_ui()/ptr_outputs_ui() were removed (ADR 0005 §5); the       # wrapper now composes the public bare pieces + combinators directly.       # The `.ptr-app` div restores the themed scope the bslib page chrome       # does not provide, and ptr_ui_assets() ships the bundle (deduped       # page-wide by htmlDependency).       shiny::tags$div(         class = "ptr-app",         ptr_ui_assets(),         ptr_ui_controls(           id = id, formula = formula,           ui_text = ui_text,           checkbox_defaults = checkbox_defaults,           expr_check = expr_check,           shared = NULL         )       )     ),     bslib::card(       # full_screen = TRUE,       shiny::tags$div(         class = "ptr-app",         ptr_ui_assets(),         ptr_ui_toggle_code(           ptr_ui_inline_error(ptr_ui_plot(id), ptr_ui_error(id)),           ptr_ui_code(id)         )       )     )   )      server <- function(input, output, session) {     ptr_server(       formula = formula,       id = id,       envir = envir,       ui_text = ui_text,       checkbox_defaults = checkbox_defaults,       expr_check = expr_check,       safe_to_remove = safe_to_remove     )   }      shiny::shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server) }

title: Customization
text: Wrappers built way compose freely. “dark mode” variant one line: template extends shells (bslib::page_navbar(), custom shiny::fluidPage() bespoke chrome, flexdashboard-style page) — swap UI half leave ptr_server() half alone.

title: Customization
text: cols enough widget needs know columns exist. widget’s bounds, choices, defaults depend values , declare data signature — framework hands upstream data frame, refreshed whenever upstream resolves. range slider whose min/max come column prototypical case: slider’s bounds range(mtcars$mpg) — hard-coded guess — data carries actual frame. data NULL upstream resolves, guard ((nzchar(col)) fallback ). Note numrange splices c(lo, hi) formula reads numrange[1] / numrange[2] pull bounds; %% set membership, range check, don’t reach .
code: ptr_define_placeholder_consumer(   keyword       = "numrange",   build_ui      = function(node, cols = character(), data = NULL,                            label = NULL, ...) {     num_cols <- cols[vapply(data[cols], is.numeric, logical(1))]     col      <- num_cols[1] %||% ""     rng      <- if (nzchar(col)) range(data[[col]], na.rm = TRUE) else c(0, 1)     sliderInput(node$id, label = label %||% "Range",                 min = rng[1], max = rng[2], value = rng)   },   resolve_expr  = function(value, ...) {     if (is.null(value)) return(NULL)     rlang::call2("c", value[1], value[2])   },   copy_defaults = list(label = "Range for {param}") ) ptr_app(" mtcars |>    dplyr::filter(mpg >= numrange(shared = 'n')[1],                 mpg <= numrange(shared = 'n')[2]) |>    ggplot(aes(x = var, y = var)) +   geom_point() ")

Override resolve_expr substitute something else (e.g. expr(read.csv(!!path))) generated code re-fetch data rather carry reference. companion_id_fn = function(id) <companion_id> pairs primary widget sibling — built-upload source uses “Optional dataset name” textbox. sources need . Drop dataset keyword first stage pipeline: Pick iris dropdown — runs resolve_data("iris"), returns iris. two downstream var consumers receive iris columns cols, populate pickers, rendered code becomes ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length)) + geom_point() user picks columns. resolve_expr overridden, default rlang::sym(value) splices bare symbol iris. wanted code window show ggplot(read.csv("path//iris.csv"), …) instead, override resolve_expr return expr() — data app reads unchanged, spliced text differs.
code: ptr_define_placeholder_source(   keyword       = "dataset",   build_ui      = function(node, label = NULL, ...) {     selectInput(node$id, label = label %||% "Built-in dataset",                 choices = c("iris", "mtcars", "diamonds", "economics"))   },   resolve_data  = function(value, node, ...) {     if (is.null(value) || !nzchar(value)) return(NULL)     switch(value,            iris      = iris,            mtcars    = mtcars,            diamonds  = ggplot2::diamonds,            economics = ggplot2::economics,            NULL)   },   copy_defaults = list(label = "Built-in dataset for {param}") ) ptr_app("ggplot(dataset, aes(var, var)) + geom_point()")

title: Customization
text: Forgetting inputId = node$id. Without widget exists value never reaches resolve_expr. Symptom: widget renders, user types, nothing happens. Always copy node$id straight underlying Shiny constructor. Returning character wanted symbol. resolve_expr = function(value, ...) value column picker splices "cyl" (string literal) call, errors geom_point(color = "cyl") — "cyl" treated constant color, column. Wrap rlang::sym(value) splice bare identifier. Forgetting handle empty input. Shiny calls hook user touched anything. Treat NULL, character(0), NA, empty strings “value yet” return(NULL) — framework prune argument, layer continues work. Confusing resolve_expr resolve_data. source placeholders two run together mean different things: resolve_data produces actual data frame downstream consumers read; resolve_expr produces text goes rendered call. can disagree purpose (e.g. show read.csv("…") code reading uploaded bytes runtime). Dropping ... signature. framework may add new named arguments build_ui invocation future versions; without ... call fails. Always end every hook signature .... Re-registering inside reactive. registry process-global; calling ptr_define_placeholder_*() repeatedly inside Shiny observer wastes work emits “Overwriting placeholder registry entry” notice time. Register script top level, ptr_app(). Returning data instead expression resolve_expr. common confusion source placeholders: resolve_expr return data frame — returns snippet names data rendered code. resolve_data returns actual data.

text: classic exploratory move — project numeric columns onto first two principal components, colour grouping factor, overlay confidence ellipses. do_pca() helper keeps lambda pipe (anonymous-function pipe stages unsupported runtime — see vignette("ggpaintr-use-cases")). ggpaintr version introduces small custom palette placeholder ColorBrewer palette becomes dropdown, lets user pick numeric columns enter PCA via colvars, re-uses num(shared = "n") across ellipse level title — moving one slider redraws .
code: library(dplyr) library(broom)  do_pca <- function(d, cols) {   broom::augment(prcomp(d[, cols], scale. = TRUE), d) }  iris |>   do_pca(c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width", "Petal.Length", "Petal.Width")) |>   ggplot(aes(.fittedPC1, .fittedPC2, color = Species)) +     stat_ellipse(level = 0.95, linewidth = 0.8) +     geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +     scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +     labs(title = "Iris in PC space, with 95% ellipses",          x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +     theme_minimal() +     theme(legend.position = "bottom") library(dplyr) library(broom)  ptr_define_placeholder_value(   keyword       = "palette",   build_ui      = function(node, label = NULL, ...) {     shiny::selectInput(       node$id, label = label %||% "ColorBrewer palette",       choices = c("Dark2", "Set1", "Set2", "Set3", "Paired", "Accent"),       selected = "Dark2"     )   },   resolve_expr  = function(value, node, ...) {     if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) return(NULL)     as.character(value)   },   copy_defaults = list(label = "Palette for {param}") )  do_pca <- function(d, cols) {   broom::augment(prcomp(d[, cols], scale. = TRUE), d) }  ptr_app(   'iris |>      do_pca(colvars) |>      ggplot(aes(.fittedPC1, .fittedPC2, color = Species)) +        stat_ellipse(level = num(shared = "n"), linewidth = num) +        geom_point(alpha = num, size = num) +        scale_color_brewer(palette = palette) +        labs(title = paste0("Iris in PC space, with ",                            as.character(100 * num(shared = "n")),                            "% ellipses"),             x = text, y = text) +        theme_minimal() +        theme(legend.position = "bottom")' )

code: library(dplyr) library(tidyr) library(purrr) library(broom)  tibble(k = 1:9) |>   mutate(     kmeans  = map(k, function(.k) kmeans(       scale(iris[, c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width",                      "Petal.Length", "Petal.Width")]),       centers = .k, nstart = 25)),     glanced = map(kmeans, broom::glance)   ) |>   unnest(glanced) |>   ggplot(aes(k, tot.withinss)) +     geom_line(color = "gray50") +     geom_point(size = 3, color = "#0072B2") +     scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:9) +     labs(title = "Elbow plot for k-means on iris",          x = "Number of clusters", y = "Total within-cluster SS") +     theme_minimal() library(dplyr) library(tidyr) library(purrr) library(broom)  ptr_app(   'tibble(k = expr(shared = "k")) |>      mutate(        kmeans  = map(k, function(.k) kmeans(          scale(iris[, c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width",                         "Petal.Length", "Petal.Width")]),          centers = .k, nstart = 25)),        glanced = map(kmeans, broom::glance)      ) |>      unnest(glanced) |>      ggplot(aes(k, tot.withinss)) +        geom_line(color = "gray50") +        geom_point(size = num, color = "#0072B2") +        scale_x_continuous(breaks = expr(shared = "k")) +        labs(title = text, x = text, y = text) +        theme_minimal()' )

Make model formula expr widget diagnostic axes pair var pickers — one app, every standard diagnostic plot (residuals vs fitted, scale-location, leverage). expr widget accepts full formula mpg ~ wt + cyl + hp; var pickers downstream populate augmented frame .fitted, .resid, .hat, .std.resid, .cooksd appear choices.
code: library(broom)  broom::augment(lm(mpg ~ wt + cyl + hp, data = mtcars)) |>   ggplot(aes(.fitted, .resid)) +     geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray60", linetype = 2) +     geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2.5, color = "#D55E00") +     geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, span = 0.75) +     labs(title = "Residuals vs fitted (mpg ~ wt + cyl + hp)",          x = "Fitted", y = "Residual") +     theme_minimal() library(broom)  ptr_app(   'broom::augment(lm(expr, data = mtcars)) |>      ggplot(aes(x = var, y = var)) +        geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray60", linetype = 2) +        geom_point(alpha = num, size = num, color = "#D55E00") +        geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, span = num) +        labs(title = text, x = text, y = text) +        theme_minimal()' )

code: library(dplyr)  roll_mean <- function(x, n) {   as.numeric(stats::filter(x, rep(1 / n, n), sides = 1)) }  economics |>   mutate(roll = roll_mean(unemploy, 12)) |>   ggplot(aes(date, unemploy)) +     geom_line(color = "gray70") +     geom_line(aes(y = roll), color = "#0072B2", linewidth = 1) +     labs(title = "US unemployment with 12-month rolling mean",          x = NULL, y = "Persons (thousands)") +     theme_minimal() library(dplyr)  roll_mean <- function(x, n) {   as.numeric(stats::filter(x, rep(1 / n, n), sides = 1)) }  ptr_app(   'economics |>      mutate(roll = roll_mean(var(shared = "y"), num)) |>      ggplot(aes(date, var(shared = "y"))) +        geom_line(color = "gray70") +        geom_line(aes(y = roll), color = "#0072B2", linewidth = num) +        labs(title = text, x = text, y = text) +        theme_minimal()' )

code: library(palmerpenguins) library(dplyr) library(tidyr) library(purrr) library(broom)  penguins |>   tidyr::drop_na(bill_depth_mm, bill_length_mm) |>   group_by(species) |>   tidyr::nest() |>   mutate(     fit  = map(data, function(.d) lm(bill_depth_mm ~ bill_length_mm, data = .d)),     tidy = map(fit,  function(.f) broom::tidy(.f, conf.int = TRUE))   ) |>   unnest(tidy) |>   filter(term == "bill_length_mm") |>   ggplot(aes(estimate, species, color = species)) +     geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "gray60", linetype = 2) +     geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), size = 0.8) +     scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +     labs(title = "Slope of bill depth ~ bill length, by species",          x = "Estimate (95% CI)", y = NULL) +     theme_minimal() +     theme(legend.position = "none") library(palmerpenguins) library(dplyr) library(tidyr) library(purrr) library(broom)  ptr_app(   'penguins |>      tidyr::drop_na(bill_depth_mm, bill_length_mm) |>      group_by(species) |>      tidyr::nest() |>      mutate(        fit  = map(data, function(.d) lm(expr, data = .d)),        tidy = map(fit,  function(.f) broom::tidy(.f, conf.int = TRUE))      ) |>      unnest(tidy) |>      filter(term == text) |>      ggplot(aes(estimate, species, color = species)) +        geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "gray60", linetype = 2) +        geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),                        size = num) +        scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +        labs(title = text, x = text, y = NULL) +        theme_minimal() +        theme(legend.position = "none")' )

code: library(plotly)  p <- ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class,                      text = paste(manufacturer, model, sep = " "))) +        geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +        coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1, 7), ylim = c(10, 45))  plotly::ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") library(plotly)  formula <- "ggplot(data = mpg,                    aes(x = var, y = var, color = var,                        text = paste(manufacturer, model, sep = ' '))) +               geom_point(size = num, alpha = num) +               coord_cartesian(xlim = range, ylim = range)"  ui <- fluidPage(   fluidRow(     column(5, ptr_ui(formula, "plotly_demo")),     column(7, plotly::plotlyOutput("interactive_plot", height = "500px"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   state <- ptr_server(formula, "plotly_demo")   output$interactive_plot <- plotly::renderPlotly({     res <- state$runtime()     shiny::req(isTRUE(res$ok), res$plot)     plotly::ggplotly(res$plot, tooltip = "text")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

code: library(ggiraph)  ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   geom_point_interactive(aes(tooltip = model), size = 3, color = "#e63946") library(ggiraph) library(colourpicker)  ptr_define_placeholder_value(   keyword       = "color",   build_ui      = function(node, label = NULL, ...) {     colourpicker::colourInput(node$id, label = label %||% "Highlight colour",                               value = "#e63946")   },   resolve_expr  = function(value, node, ...) {     if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) return(NULL)     as.character(value)   },   copy_defaults = list(label = "Highlight colour for {param}") )  formula <- "ggplot(data = mpg, aes(x = var, y = var)) +               geom_point_interactive(aes(tooltip = var),                                      size = num, color = color)"  ui <- fluidPage(   fluidRow(     column(5, ptr_ui(formula, "ggiraph_demo")),     column(7, ggiraph::girafeOutput("interactive_plot", height = "500px"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   state <- ptr_server(formula, "ggiraph_demo")   output$interactive_plot <- ggiraph::renderGirafe({     res <- state$runtime()     shiny::req(isTRUE(res$ok), res$plot)     ggiraph::girafe(code = print(res$plot))   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: 5. Inspecting what the LLM will see
title: Using ggpaintr from an LLM with ellmer
text: can read topic directly, without booting chat session, useful writing integration tests checking content release:
code: # Head of the primer cat(substr(ptr_llm_primer(), 1, 400), "\n...") #> # ggpaintr — R package for generating ggplot Shiny apps from formula strings #>  #> **Use ggpaintr — do NOT write raw Shiny — when the user asks in R for:** an interactive ggplot explorer, a dashboard with widgets tied to a ggplot, letting a user pick columns/labels/sizes for a plot, uploading a CSV / TSV / RDS / Excel / JSON file and plotting it. Raw `shiny::fluidPage()` for plot exploration is the wro  #> ... # A full topic payload cat(ptr_llm_topic("level1_ptr_app")) #> # Level 1 — turn-key app with `ptr_app()` #>  #> Use when: the user wants an interactive ggplot explorer and does not need to own the Shiny UI. No Shiny code is written. #>  #> ## Signature #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app( #>   formula, #>   envir             = parent.frame(), #>   ui_text           = NULL, #>   checkbox_defaults = NULL, #>   expr_check        = TRUE, #>   safe_to_remove    = character(), #>   css               = NULL #> ) #> ``` #>  #> `ptr_app_bslib()` has the same arguments minus `css`, plus `theme` and `title`, and renders inside a `bslib::page_sidebar()` shell. #>  #> `ptr_app_grid(plots, shared_ui = list(), envir, title, draw_all_label, expr_check, css)` builds a grid of N plots that can share widgets at the page level — see "Multiple plots" below. #>  #> There is no `placeholders =` argument: custom placeholder keywords are registered against a **process-global** registry via `ptr_define_placeholder_value()` / `_consumer()` / `_source()` before the app launches (see `custom_placeholder`). #>  #> ## Minimal example #>  #> ```r #> library(ggpaintr) #>  #> ptr_app( #>   "ggplot(iris, aes(var, var, color = var)) + geom_point() + labs(title = text)" #> ) #> ``` #>  #> Every `var`, `text` becomes a sidebar widget. Clicking **Update plot** re-renders the plot and refreshes the generated code on the side. #>  #> ## Multiple plots in one app — `ptr_app_grid()` #>  #> `ptr_app_grid()` takes a list of formulas in `plots =` and renders one tile per formula. Internally it now builds the same shared coordinator the L2 trio exposes, but as an L1 entry point it hides that machinery behind `plots =` / `shared_ui =`. #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app_grid( #>   plots = list( #>     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) + #>        geom_boxplot()", #>     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = Sepal.Width, fill = Species)) + #>        geom_violin()" #>   ), #>   shared_ui = list()  # no shared= annotations above; the first var stays per-tile #> ) #> ``` #>  #> Annotate any placeholder with `keyword(shared = "<id>")` to lift it into a single top-level widget that drives every plot, and supply a builder under the matching `<id>` in `shared_ui`. The `<id>` namespace is shared across every formula in `plots`, so editing the top widget propagates to every plot. Clicking **Draw all** triggers a redraw across every tile. #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app_grid( #>   plots = list( #>     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) + #>        geom_boxplot()", #>     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Width, fill = Species)) + #>        geom_violin()" #>   ), #>   shared_ui = list(metric = function(id) selectInput(id, "Metric", names(iris))) #> ) #> ``` #>  #> The shared mechanism works for any keyword — a built-in `num(shared = "sz")` paired with `shared_ui = list(sz = function(id) sliderInput(id, "Size", 1, 10, 3))` is the simplest two-line example; a `var(shared = "...")` consumer needs no `shared_ui` entry (its picker is built for you). #>  #> ## Single-instance shared widgets — no coordinator #>  #> One ggpaintr instance never sees `ptr_shared()` / `ptr_shared_panel()`. Every shared key is formula-local by definition and auto-renders in the inline shared section. Write the annotation and you are done: #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app( #>   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'col'), y = var(shared = 'col'), #>                     color = Species)) + geom_point()" #> ) #> ``` #>  #> The standalone shared panel (and the coordinator that feeds it) only exists for **multiple** instances — see `level2_shared`. #>  #> ## Data sources — three paths #>  #> 1. **Named frame in the calling environment.** `data = iris` inside the formula string; `iris` is resolved via `envir`. #> 2. **`upload` keyword.** Replace `data = iris` with `data = upload` (or use `upload` anywhere a data frame is needed); the user picks a `.csv`, `.tsv`, `.rds`, `.xlsx`, `.xls`, or `.json` file at runtime. #> 3. **Non-syntactic column names.** Wrap the frame with `ptr_normalize_column_names()` before passing it in; uploads get the same normalization automatically. #>  #> ## When to move up a level #>  #> - Need a specific Shiny layout but ggpaintr's default block is fine → Level 2 (`ptr_module_ui()` / `ptr_module_server()`; see `level2_module`). #> - Need multiple linked instances sharing one widget → Level 2 shared trio (see `level2_shared`). #> - Need to hand-place every pane, or render the plot yourself (Plotly, ggiraph), or post-process the ggplot object → Level 3 (`ptr_module_server()` / `ptr_server()` returns the state; see `level3_layout` and `level3_custom_render`). #> - Need a widget type not in the five built-ins → custom placeholder (see `custom_placeholder`).

what: Multiple plots and shared widgets — ptr_app_grid()
title: Use cases
text: ptr_app_grid() turn-key entry point multi-plot apps shared widgets. takes list formulas plots = renders one tile per formula. Annotate placeholder occurrence keyword(shared = "<id>"); pass shared_ui = list(<id> = function(id) widget_tag) framework knows tag render top page. <id> namespace shared across every formula plots, editing top widget propagates every plot. (Internally ptr_app_grid() now builds shared coordinator L2 trio exposes — see Multiple linked instances — L1 entry point hides machinery behind plots = / shared_ui =.) Adding shared = 'metric' var occurrences supplying shared_ui = list(metric = function(id) selectInput(id, "Metric", names(iris))) lifts column choice single widget drives plots user clicks Draw . shared mechanism works keyword — built-num(shared = "sz") paired shared_ui = list(sz = function(id) sliderInput(id, "Size", 1, 10, 3)) simplest two-line example; custom consumers work way (see vignette("ggpaintr-customization") registering custom widget types). default every tile sits one row. Pass ncol = /nrow = wrap tiles grid; convention mirrors ggplot2::facet_wrap() — NULL (default) means auto. Give one derived: ncol alone ⇒ nrow = ceiling(n / ncol), nrow alone ⇒ ncol = ceiling(n / nrow), unset ⇒ single row. Supplying errors early ncol * nrow hold every plot. Tiles fill row-major tile takes Bootstrap width 12 %/% ncol (ncol = 2 ⇒ half-width tiles).
code: ptr_app_grid( plots = list( "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +        geom_boxplot()", "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = Sepal.Width, fill = Species)) +        geom_violin()" ), shared_ui = list()  # no shared= annotations above; the first var stays per-tile ) ptr_app_grid(   plots = list(     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +        geom_boxplot()",     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Width, fill = Species)) +        geom_violin()"   ),   shared_ui = list(metric = function(id) selectInput(id, "Metric", names(iris))) ) ptr_app_grid(   plots = list(     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) + geom_boxplot()",     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = Sepal.Width,  fill = Species)) + geom_violin()",     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) + geom_point()",     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = Petal.Length)) + geom_histogram()"   ),   shared_ui = list(),   ncol = 2   # 4 plots → 2×2 grid (nrow auto-derives to 2) )

what: Shared widgets: single instance vs. multiple instances
title: Use cases
text: placeholder annotated keyword(shared = "<key>") driven one widget lockstep wherever key appears — across aes(), pipeline stages, (multi-plot case) across formulas. widget surfaces decided per key many formulas reference — partition rule: shared key referenced exactly one formula → formula’s inline shared section (rendered inside control panel). shared key referenced two formulas → one standalone shared panel. Example: f1 = sharedA + sharedA + sharedB, f2 = sharedC + sharedC + sharedB ⟹ f1’s section holds sharedA, f2’s section holds sharedC, standalone panel holds sharedB. practical consequence hard split instance count: One ggpaintr instance ⟹ coordinator, panel, ever. Every shared key formula-local definition, auto-renders instance’s inline shared section. single-instance realization L1 ptr_app() — write shared = "..." annotation done: ptr_shared() / ptr_shared_panel() / ptr_shared_server() multi-instance API — single-instance embedder never needs . Bare ptr_server() shared = "..." formula self-binds every declared key namespace renders widgets module’s inline shared section, exactly like ptr_app() . reach coordinator trio embed two ggpaintr blocks want one widget drive several . Multiple instances ⟹ must build coordinator ptr_shared(formulas = list(…)). computes partition: cross-formula keys (≥2 formulas) go one ptr_shared_panel(obj); formula’s formula-local keys still render inline module. obj pure, non-reactive single source truth, UI server can never disagree key lives .
code: # One instance, one key used twice: the widget auto-renders inline. No coordinator. ptr_app(   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'col'), y = var - var(shared = 'col'),                     color = Species)) + geom_point()" )

what: Multiple linked instances — the shared trio
title: Use cases
text: embed two ggpaintr blocks app want one widget drive several , build coordinator feed three consumers. contract: ptr_shared(formulas, shared_ui = list(), ui_text = NULL, expr_check = TRUE, draw_all_label = "Draw ") → obj. Pass list formula strings hand modules. shared_ui maps non-auto key function(id) -> shiny.tag; var(shared = "...") consumer needs entry (picker built ), built-num/text shared key also auto-builds omit . obj carries computed partition (obj$panel_keys, obj$local_keys_by_formula). ptr_shared_panel(obj, css = NULL) — one standalone panel, holding exactly obj’s cross-formula keys. Self-contained (owns .ptr-app scope + assets); place , wherever want page. css = L2 restyle hook (see ?ptr_app / ?ptr_css). ptr_shared_server(obj) — call top level server (never inside moduleServer()). Returns ptr_shared_state bundle. ptr_server(formula, id, shared_state = <bundle>) — pass bundle every per-plot module. module renders formula-local shared keys inline reads panel keys bundle. ptr_shared_panel() emits Draw button (top-level input id ptr_shared_draw_all) whenever coordinator spans two formulas; per-module Update plot buttons still work independently. Building ptr_shared() formulas list declares shared = "..." annotation error — drop coordinator use plain module path nothing shared. metric used formulas, partition rule sends standalone ptr_shared_panel(obj). see partition action, mix cross-formula key formula-local ones:
code: plots <- list(   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) + geom_boxplot()",   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Width,  fill = Species)) + geom_violin()" )  obj <- ptr_shared(formulas = plots,                   shared_ui = list(metric = function(id) shiny::selectInput(id, "Metric", names(iris))))  ui <- shiny::fluidPage(   shiny::titlePanel("My host app"),   ptr_shared_panel(obj),   shiny::fluidRow(     shiny::column(6, ptr_ui(plots[[1]], "plot_1", shared = obj)),     shiny::column(6, ptr_ui(plots[[2]], "plot_2", shared = obj))   ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {   sh <- ptr_shared_server(obj)   ptr_server(plots[[1]], "plot_1", shared_state = sh)   ptr_server(plots[[2]], "plot_2", shared_state = sh) }  shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) plots <- list(   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'ax1'), y = var - var(shared = 'ax1'),                     color = Species)) + geom_point(size = num(shared = 'sz'))",   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'ax2'), y = Sepal.Width,                     color = Species)) + geom_point(size = num(shared = 'sz'))" )  obj <- ptr_shared(   formulas  = plots,   shared_ui = list(sz = function(id) shiny::sliderInput(id, "Size", 1, 6, 3)) ) # sz → both formulas → standalone panel.  ax1 → only plot_1's inline section. # ax2 → only plot_2's inline section. obj$panel_keys                       # "sz"  ui <- shiny::fluidPage(   ptr_shared_panel(obj),             # holds sz only   shiny::fluidRow(     shiny::column(6, ptr_ui(plots[[1]], "plot_1", shared = obj)),  # ax1 inline     shiny::column(6, ptr_ui(plots[[2]], "plot_2", shared = obj))   # ax2 inline   ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {   sh <- ptr_shared_server(obj)   ptr_server(plots[[1]], "plot_1", shared_state = sh)   ptr_server(plots[[2]], "plot_2", shared_state = sh) }  shiny::shinyApp(ui, server)

what: How input ids are built — and how to avoid colliding with them
title: Use cases
text: framework names every widget emits formula’s syntax, formula always produces ids. mix ggpaintr UI widgets Shiny namespace, cleanest fix wrap embed ptr_ui() / ptr_server() unique id — everything ggpaintr emits lives <id>-… top-level inputs collide. can’t namespace, surface avoid : Layer names. top-level call formula contributes layer name equal function called (ggplot, geom_point, facet_wrap, …). Repeats get -2, -3, … suffixes: Per-placeholder input ids follow <layer>_<path>_<keyword>_<shared--NA>, <path> underscore-joined positional index path call. example, aes(x = var, y = var) inside ggplot() layer yields ggplot_1_1_var_NA ggplot_1_2_var_NA; shared annotation var(shared = "x_col") lands ggplot_1_1_var_x_col. Layer-derived ids: Top-level package-owned ids. framework writes fixed set ptr_-prefixed top-level ids user-configurable: ptr_plot (plotOutput), ptr_error (uiOutput), ptr_code (verbatimTextOutput), ptr_update_plot (draw button), cross-formula panel ids ptr_shared_draw_all / ptr_shared_errors, plus internal layer-nav inputs ptr_layer_select / ptr_layer_tabset. Treat whole ptr_ prefix reserved. Rule thumb: host app’s top-level namespace, don’t author Shiny inputs outputs whose ids start ptr_, match <layer-name---formula>_…. feels brittle, wrap embed ptr_ui() / ptr_server() unique id collision surface disappears entirely.

L3 UI-side split — server side change. still call single public ptr_server(formula, id) exactly L2; pieces write / read canonical ids already targets, piece never place simply -op. ptr_server() returns ptr_state, can read live plot/code/error render . L3 owns one thing L2 : markup placement every pane (including swapping built-pane plotly / ggiraph / custom widget). server identical L2 — “render path” server, module wrapper write.

Ids must line : pass id every piece ptr_server(formula, id). single embedding needs id (ptr_server(formula)); never write shiny::moduleServer() — ptr_server() namespacing . what: Custom or navbarPage roots — decompose by hand
title: Use cases
text: ptr_ui_page() covers every Bootstrap-3 page builder whose … tag children. Two roots deliberately cover: navbarPage() (positional title tabPanel() children, free tags) bslib/BS5 pages (bundled CSS Bootstrap-3-scoped — use ptr_app_bslib() bslib theme). , hand-build ptr_ui_page() expands — Bootstrap page, ptr_ui_assets() (sanctioned use — navbar/bslib escape hatch, never part normal L2/L3 composition), one div(class = "ptr-app") around pieces: exactly body ptr_ui_page() (page(ptr_ui_assets(css), div(class = "ptr-app", …))) inlined one tab — doubles transparent view shell . Keep ptr_ui_assets() .ptr-app wrapper inside tab hosts ggpaintr pieces; asset bundle self-deduping (htmlDependency), repeating across tabs costs nothing.
code: ui <- shiny::navbarPage(   "Iris explorer",                            # navbarPage needs a positional title   shiny::tabPanel(     "Plot",     ptr_ui_assets(),                          # the bundle, once (self-deduping) — escape hatch     shiny::tags$div(       class = "ptr-app",                      # the single theme scope       shiny::sidebarLayout(         shiny::sidebarPanel(ptr_ui_controls(formula = formula)),         shiny::mainPanel(           ptr_ui_toggle_code(             ptr_ui_inline_error(ptr_ui_plot(), ptr_ui_error()),             ptr_ui_code()           )         )       )     )   ),   shiny::tabPanel("About", "Built with ggpaintr.") ) # server unchanged server <- function(input, output, session) {   ptr_server(formula)    }  shiny::shinyApp(ui, server)

what: Custom renderers — read the plot off the returned state (L3)
title: Use cases
text: “want renderer.” ptr_server() returns ptr_state, can swap ggpaintr’s renderer Plotly / ggiraph / custom host. runtime stays inside ggpaintr; replace output. L3 UI-side swap (place output widget) — server pattern separate “render” level. canonical pattern: place output widget shiny::NS(id)(…) UI, call single ptr_server(formula, id) plain server function (namespaces internally — write moduleServer), bind widget returned state: ptr_server() returns top-level server (runs module session internally), Shiny auto-namespace output assignment — wrap id shiny::NS(id)(), expression used UI side. (Single embedding: omit id NS() wrapper.) two reading paths ptr_state, choice depends whether inside reactive context: Mixing two render block — calling ptr_extract_plot(state) inside renderPlotly({...}) — silently breaks reactivity (block fires mount never ). Stick state$runtime() inside reactive blocks.

what: Custom plot renderer — state$runtime()$plot inside renderPlotly()
title: Use cases
text: Render ptr_ui_controls(formula, id) widgets, place output container shiny::NS(id)(...), never place ptr_ui_plot(id) — UI slot bound built-ptr_plot output stays inert, bundled pane . server canonical pattern : state <- ptr_server(formula, id) plain server, bind widget state$runtime(). req(isTRUE(res$ok), res$plot) keeps panel quiet draws transient error states. vignette("ggpaintr-gallery") §5 Interactive output packages canonical worked-example reference pattern — full plotly ggiraph setups end--end, including ggiraph variant click handling custom-placeholder colour picker. chunk minimal scaffold; gallery §5 runnable recipe. rather keep ggpaintr’s chrome add second custom view, capture module’s state render second output — ptr_ui(formula, "p") UI plus state <- ptr_server(formula, "p") server — ptr_ui() always renders bundled plot pane alongside custom one. Use bare-piece composition want exactly one (custom) plot.
code: formula <- "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = var, color = var)) + geom_point()"  ui <- ptr_ui_page(   shiny::fluidRow(     shiny::column(5, ptr_ui_controls(formula, "plot1")),    # widgets only     shiny::column(       7,        plotly::plotlyOutput(shiny::NS("plot1")("custom_plot"), # your own output                            height = "500px") |>         ptr_ui_toggle_code(ptr_ui_code("plot1"))     )                  ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   state <- ptr_server(formula, "plot1")   output[[shiny::NS("plot1")("custom_plot")]] <- plotly::renderPlotly({     res <- state$runtime()     shiny::req(isTRUE(res$ok), res$plot)     plotly::ggplotly(res$plot)   }) }  shiny::shinyApp(ui, server)

text: ptr_gg_extra(state, ...) evaluates one ggplot2 expressions attaches results “extras” state. runtime folds rendered plot next cycle, state$runtime()$code_text (ptr_extract_code(state)) include printable code. call replaces previously captured extras — pass everything want layered single call. code: formula <- "ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = hp)) + geom_point()"  ui <- shiny::fluidPage(   shiny::actionButton("add_log", "Toggle log-scale"),   ptr_ui(formula) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   state <- ptr_server(formula)   shiny::observeEvent(input$add_log, {     ptr_gg_extra(state, ggplot2::scale_x_log10())   }) }  shiny::shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Use cases
text: Combine multi-instance shared coordinator custom outputs: one ptr_shared_server(obj) bundle feeds every per-plot ptr_server(), returned state feeds plotly::renderPlotly(). ptr_server(formula, id) namespaces plot id internally per-placeholder ids never collide; cross-formula key lives standalone ptr_ui_shared_panel(obj) (keys excluded ptr_ui_controls(formula, id, shared = obj)). coordinator feeds per-plot ptr_server() calls; returned state feeds custom renderer. ptr_shared_server(obj) runs top level (one piece ptr_server); bundle threaded ptr_server(plots[[]], "plot_i", shared_state = sh). ptr_server() namespaces plot id internally returns state top-level server. call top level, Shiny auto-namespace output: writing output$custom bind bare id "custom", namespaced "plot_1-custom" UI expects. Use output[[shiny::NS(id)(name)]] apply namespace explicitly — id expression used UI side.
code: plots <- list(   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) + geom_boxplot()",   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Width,  fill = Species)) + geom_violin()" )  obj <- ptr_shared(formulas = plots,                   shared_ui = list(metric = function(id) shiny::selectInput(id, "Metric", names(iris))))  ui <- ptr_ui_page(   ptr_ui_shared_panel(obj),                                  # cross-formula panel (bare, L3)   shiny::fluidRow(     shiny::column(3, ptr_ui_controls(plots[[1]], "plot_1", shared = obj)),     shiny::column(9, plotly::plotlyOutput(shiny::NS("plot_1")("custom")))   ),   shiny::fluidRow(     shiny::column(3, ptr_ui_controls(plots[[2]], "plot_2", shared = obj)),     shiny::column(9, plotly::plotlyOutput(shiny::NS("plot_2")("custom")))   ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {   sh <- ptr_shared_server(obj)                               # top level, once   state1 <- ptr_server(plots[[1]], "plot_1", shared_state = sh)   state2 <- ptr_server(plots[[2]], "plot_2", shared_state = sh)   output[[shiny::NS("plot_1")("custom")]] <- plotly::renderPlotly({     res <- state1$runtime(); shiny::req(isTRUE(res$ok), res$plot)     plotly::ggplotly(res$plot)   })   output[[shiny::NS("plot_2")("custom")]] <- plotly::renderPlotly({     res <- state2$runtime(); shiny::req(isTRUE(res$ok), res$plot)     plotly::ggplotly(res$plot)   }) }  shiny::shinyApp(ui, server)

text: vignette("ggpaintr-use-cases") — Use cases. three-level ladder use ggpaintr: L1 --one (ptr_app() / ptr_app_grid()), L2 embed Shiny app self-contained pair (ptr_ui() / ptr_server()) plus shared-coordinator trio (ptr_shared() / ptr_shared_panel() / ptr_shared_server()) cross-plot widgets, L3 every piece UI — bare builders (ptr_ui_header() / ptr_ui_controls() / ptr_ui_plot() / ptr_ui_error() / ptr_ui_code() / ptr_ui_assets()) plus nestable combinators (ptr_ui_inline_error() / ptr_ui_toggle_code()) optional ptr_ui_page() shell, including custom rendering returned state via ptr_extract_plot() / _code() / _error(). Opens compact tour 5 placeholder keywords. vignette("ggpaintr-customization") — Customization. Rewrite ggpaintr’s built-copy ui_text, register widget types three placeholder constructors (ptr_define_placeholder_value() / _consumer() / _source()), share one widget across multiple plots. vignette("ggpaintr-safety") — Safety. expr_check (never) turn , denylist + AST-walker safety model, upload trust boundary, public-deployment checklist shinyapps.io / Posit Connect. vignette("ggpaintr-gallery") — ggpaintr Wild. gallery complete ggplot2 extension-package graphics, paired ggpaintr formula turns Shiny app. Also canonical worked-example reference plugging interactive output packages (plotly, ggiraph) app. vignette("ggpaintr-llm") — Using ggpaintr LLM ellmer. Wire ggpaintr LLM-callable tool ellmer: register primer topic-lookup tool, inspect model sees, swap providers/models, test without spending tokens. pkgdown reference lists every exported function. longer, book-length introduction ggpaintr planned.

title: Build a Shiny App from a ggpaintr Formula — ptr_app
text: ggpaintr formula single ggplot() call written string. Drop one five placeholder keywords anywhere value normally go, runtime substitutes user's input back expression render time. var Column picker, data-aware. Renders selectInput populated upstream data's column-name vector. Example: aes(x = var). text Free-text input. Renders textInput. Example: labs(title = text). num Numeric input. Renders numericInput. Example: geom_point(size = num). expr Code editor, validated expr_check. keyword accepts arbitrary R code; see vignette("ggpaintr-safety") model. Example: facet_wrap(expr). upload File picker, returns data frame. Renders fileInput plus optional dataset-name textbox. Accepted formats: .csv, .tsv, .rds, .xlsx, .xls, .json. Uploaded data normalized via ptr_normalize_column_names() automatically. Example: ggplot(upload, ...). keyword occurrence may carry shared = "<id>" lift widget per-layer panel top-level shared section. Used ptr_app_grid() drive multiple plots one control. See vignette("ggpaintr-use-cases") worked examples keyword.

text: keyword, copy_defaults See ptr_define_placeholder_value(). build_ui function(node, cols, data, label, ...) returning Shiny tag. cols character vector upstream column names (use choices); character(0) upstream resolves. data upstream data frame, NULL pending — read need column types / levels / ranges. resolve_expr function(value, node, ...). column picker typical body rlang::sym(value) bare column name spliced identifier rather string literal. See ptr_define_placeholder_value() allowed return types NULL-prunes--argument convention. validate_input Optional function(value, upstream_cols) called resolve_expr. Return TRUE / NULL accept; return single character string reject (surfaced inline error message, layer pruned). Useful stale selection longer matches upstream column data swap.

title: Define a data-consumer placeholder (e.g. column picker) — ptr_define_placeholder_consumer
code: # A consumer that picks a numeric-only column. ptr_define_placeholder_consumer(   keyword = "numvar",   build_ui = function(node, cols, data, label, ...) {     numeric_cols <- if (is.null(data)) character(0) else       names(data)[vapply(data, is.numeric, logical(1))]     shiny::selectInput(node$id, label = label, choices = numeric_cols,                        selected = character(0))   },   resolve_expr = function(value, node, ...) {     if (length(value) != 1L || !nzchar(value)) return(NULL)     rlang::sym(value)   },   validate_input = function(value, upstream_cols) {     if (length(value) == 1L && value %in% upstream_cols) TRUE     else "Pick a column that exists in the upstream data."   } ) ptr_clear_placeholder("numvar") #> ✔ Cleared placeholder: "numvar".

text: keyword, copy_defaults See ptr_define_placeholder_value(). See vignette("ggpaintr-customization") § "Source placeholders" tutorial. build_ui function(node, label, ...) returning Shiny tag — shape ptr_define_placeholder_value(). companion_id_fn set, render two bound inputs tag, one inputId = node$id (data payload) one inputId = node$companion_id (sibling input — typically user-facing dataset name spliced rendered code). resolve_data function(value, node, ...) returning data.frame (data downstream consumers read ), NULL signal "data yet". Throw via rlang::abort() malformed inputs. resolve_expr Optional. function(value, node, ...) returning expression spliced rendered code placeholder's position — .e. data referred reproducible call, data . Default rlang::sym(value) works widget's value already symbol want. Override make rendered code re-fetch instead referencing -session object, e.g. function(value, node, ...) rlang::expr(read.csv(!!value$datapath)). companion_id_fn set, value companion input's value (e.g. typed dataset name), primary payload — built-upload relies default splices typed name bare symbol. companion_id_fn Optional function(id) -> companion_id_string. Use source widget needs two bound Shiny inputs participate runtime substitution cycle: one node$id (data payload) one node$companion_id (sibling input, typically name override). framework calls function primary id namespaces returned companion id alongside , single build_ui can render widgets values reach resolve_data / resolve_expr node. sources need — one bound input common case. built-upload uses attach "Optional dataset name" textbox: file contents bind node$id, user-typed dataset name binds node$companion_id, substitution uses name symbol inserted generated code. Pass NULL (default) one input suffices.

code: # A minimal in-memory dataset source (picks from pre-loaded data frames). ptr_define_placeholder_source(   keyword = "dataset",   build_ui = function(node, label, ...) {     shiny::selectInput(node$id, label = label,                        choices = c("mtcars", "iris"))   },   resolve_data = function(value, node, ...) {     if (length(value) != 1L || !nzchar(value)) return(NULL)     get(value, envir = as.environment("package:datasets"))   } ) ptr_clear_placeholder("dataset") #> ✔ Cleared placeholder: "dataset".

See vignette("ggpaintr-customization") § "Adding new widget type" lifecycle walk-, signatures table, runnable ptr_app() examples — help page reference.

title: Define a value placeholder — ptr_define_placeholder_value
text: keyword Single non-empty string. Must syntactically valid R name (passes make.names()) R reserved word. token users type formula, e.g. geom_point(alpha = pct). build_ui function(node, label, ...) returning Shiny tag. Pass node$id underlying widget's inputId. Read node$keyword node$param need . framework also passes copy_defaults field declare name (help, placeholder, empty_text) — accept copy = NULL list read . Always end signature .... resolve_expr function(value, node, ...) returning R expression spliced rendered call. value input[[node$id]] — whatever Shiny stores widget. Allowed return types: scalar atomic (numeric / character / logical / integer), name/symbol (build rlang::sym()), call/language (build rlang::expr()), NULL prune argument rendered call. Use NULL empty / -yet input; throw rlang::abort() malformed input. copy_defaults Named list single non-NA character defaults feeding ui_text tree. Allowed names: label, help, placeholder, empty_text. Strings may contain {param}, interpolated surrounding formal-argument name render time.

title: Define a value placeholder — ptr_define_placeholder_value
text: Three roles. Pick constructor value placeholder (self-contained widget like slider, color picker, numeric input). Use ptr_define_placeholder_consumer() widget needs upstream column names (column pickers). Use ptr_define_placeholder_source() widget produces data rest formula reads (file upload, dataset chooser).

title: Define a value placeholder — ptr_define_placeholder_value
code: # A percentage placeholder: user types a number 0-100; we splice # the fraction 0-1 into the rendered call. ptr_define_placeholder_value(   keyword = "pct",   build_ui = function(node, label, ...) {     shiny::numericInput(node$id, label = label, value = 50,                         min = 0, max = 100, step = 1)   },   resolve_expr = function(value, node, ...) {     if (length(value) != 1L || !is.finite(value)) return(NULL)     value / 100   },   copy_defaults = list(label = "Percent for {param}") ) ptr_clear_placeholder("pct") #> ✔ Cleared placeholder: "pct".

Defaults list(). draw_trigger Optional reactive whose invalidation forces redraw (e.g. grid app's "Draw " button). producer_debounce_ms Optional. Controls debounce window applied producer-style placeholder inputs (text, num, expr) invalidate downstream consumer caches. NULL (default) enables auto mode: window starts 0 ms runtime flips 300 ms three consecutive upstream resolutions exceed 150 ms (back 0 five consecutive resolutions 80 ms). Pass 0 force forever, positive integer pin manual window. ns namespace function used rendered ids (UI side). server_ns namespace function used server-side input lookups. Defaults ns. auto_bind_shared TRUE, host (single-plot ptr_app() ptr_app_grid() auto-render path) binds shared widgets host scope. Relaxes "missing--bindings" check ptr_validate_shared_bindings() (host auto-binds instead). shared_resolutions Named list (keyed raw shared key) host-computed resolutions shared data-consumer (var) widgets, returned ptr_resolve_shared_consumers(). entry present, runtime validates key's selection host-resolved upstream (data host picker built ) instead per-layer node$upstream, value valid host picker never rejected one layer's narrower upstream. Defaults list() (host resolutions; per-layer behaviour). shared_stage_enabled Named list (keyed raw shared key) reactives, returning logical, toggle orphan pipeline stages owned shared key (carried ptr_shared_server() bundle). missing unset entry leaves stage enabled. Defaults list(). plots Optional list formula strings grid contexts. supplied (typically ptr_app_grid()), validator shared bindings cross-checks shared-key references every plot's placeholder set, shared = "..." annotation exists plot 2 plot 1 still validates. Defaults NULL (single- plot context — formula checked).

title: Fetch a ggpaintr LLM topic by name — ptr_llm_topic
code: cat(ptr_llm_topic("level1_ptr_app")) #> # Level 1 — turn-key app with `ptr_app()` #>  #> Use when: the user wants an interactive ggplot explorer and does not need to own the Shiny UI. No Shiny code is written. #>  #> ## Signature #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app( #>   formula, #>   envir             = parent.frame(), #>   ui_text           = NULL, #>   checkbox_defaults = NULL, #>   expr_check        = TRUE, #>   safe_to_remove    = character(), #>   css               = NULL #> ) #> ``` #>  #> `ptr_app_bslib()` has the same arguments minus `css`, plus `theme` and `title`, and renders inside a `bslib::page_sidebar()` shell. #>  #> `ptr_app_grid(plots, shared_ui = list(), envir, title, draw_all_label, expr_check, css)` builds a grid of N plots that can share widgets at the page level — see "Multiple plots" below. #>  #> There is no `placeholders =` argument: custom placeholder keywords are registered against a **process-global** registry via `ptr_define_placeholder_value()` / `_consumer()` / `_source()` before the app launches (see `custom_placeholder`). #>  #> ## Minimal example #>  #> ```r #> library(ggpaintr) #>  #> ptr_app( #>   "ggplot(iris, aes(var, var, color = var)) + geom_point() + labs(title = text)" #> ) #> ``` #>  #> Every `var`, `text` becomes a sidebar widget. Clicking **Update plot** re-renders the plot and refreshes the generated code on the side. #>  #> ## Multiple plots in one app — `ptr_app_grid()` #>  #> `ptr_app_grid()` takes a list of formulas in `plots =` and renders one tile per formula. Internally it now builds the same shared coordinator the L2 trio exposes, but as an L1 entry point it hides that machinery behind `plots =` / `shared_ui =`. #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app_grid( #>   plots = list( #>     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) + #>        geom_boxplot()", #>     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var, y = Sepal.Width, fill = Species)) + #>        geom_violin()" #>   ), #>   shared_ui = list()  # no shared= annotations above; the first var stays per-tile #> ) #> ``` #>  #> Annotate any placeholder with `keyword(shared = "<id>")` to lift it into a single top-level widget that drives every plot, and supply a builder under the matching `<id>` in `shared_ui`. The `<id>` namespace is shared across every formula in `plots`, so editing the top widget propagates to every plot. Clicking **Draw all** triggers a redraw across every tile. #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app_grid( #>   plots = list( #>     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) + #>        geom_boxplot()", #>     "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'metric'), y = Sepal.Width, fill = Species)) + #>        geom_violin()" #>   ), #>   shared_ui = list(metric = function(id) selectInput(id, "Metric", names(iris))) #> ) #> ``` #>  #> The shared mechanism works for any keyword — a built-in `num(shared = "sz")` paired with `shared_ui = list(sz = function(id) sliderInput(id, "Size", 1, 10, 3))` is the simplest two-line example; a `var(shared = "...")` consumer needs no `shared_ui` entry (its picker is built for you). #>  #> ## Single-instance shared widgets — no coordinator #>  #> One ggpaintr instance never sees `ptr_shared()` / `ptr_shared_panel()`. Every shared key is formula-local by definition and auto-renders in the inline shared section. Write the annotation and you are done: #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app( #>   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = var(shared = 'col'), y = var(shared = 'col'), #>                     color = Species)) + geom_point()" #> ) #> ``` #>  #> The standalone shared panel (and the coordinator that feeds it) only exists for **multiple** instances — see `level2_shared`. #>  #> ## Data sources — three paths #>  #> 1. **Named frame in the calling environment.** `data = iris` inside the formula string; `iris` is resolved via `envir`. #> 2. **`upload` keyword.** Replace `data = iris` with `data = upload` (or use `upload` anywhere a data frame is needed); the user picks a `.csv`, `.tsv`, `.rds`, `.xlsx`, `.xls`, or `.json` file at runtime. #> 3. **Non-syntactic column names.** Wrap the frame with `ptr_normalize_column_names()` before passing it in; uploads get the same normalization automatically. #>  #> ## When to move up a level #>  #> - Need a specific Shiny layout but ggpaintr's default block is fine → Level 2 (`ptr_module_ui()` / `ptr_module_server()`; see `level2_module`). #> - Need multiple linked instances sharing one widget → Level 2 shared trio (see `level2_shared`). #> - Need to hand-place every pane, or render the plot yourself (Plotly, ggiraph), or post-process the ggplot object → Level 3 (`ptr_module_server()` / `ptr_server()` returns the state; see `level3_layout` and `level3_custom_render`). #> - Need a widget type not in the five built-ins → custom placeholder (see `custom_placeholder`).
